addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/slides/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → FigJam file, use get_figjam; pass the original board URL as figjamUrl when available
- figma.com/slides/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → Figma Slides file

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

Check the t… [truncated]

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- pr-summary: Summarize changes in a pull request
- api-conventions: API design patterns for this codebase
- explain-code: Explains code with visual diagrams and analogies. Use when explaining how code works, teaching about a codebase, or when the user asks "how does this work?"
- migrate-component: Migrate a component from one framework to another
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.thinking:
The user is reporting two bugs in transform scripts related to the GeMeA project. Let me look at the relevant files to understand the issues.

1. The URN fix didn't carry over - a subject URI is missing the `urn:ddb:` prefix (or similar), resulting in a bare ID instead of a proper URI
2. The gemea resource emits `edm:hasMet` which it shouldn't

Let me explore the transform scripts to understand the codebase.

content.content:
1	"""Utility functions: N-Quads formatting, URI minting, value normalisation."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import re
6	from collections import Counter
7	
8	from .constants import (
9	    AgentDict, NQuad, NQList,
10	    GEMEA_BASE, DDB_ITEM_BASE,
11	    _PREFIXES, _MEDIATYPE_PREFIX, _SECTOR_PREFIX,
12	)
13	
14	# Characters forbidden inside N-Triples IRI references (RFC 3987 + NT spec)
15	_IRI_UNSAFE_RE = re.compile(r'[\x00-\x20<>"{}|\\^`\x7f]')
16	
17	
18	def _sanitize_iri(iri: str) -> str:
19	    """Percent-encode characters illegal inside NT IRI references."""
20	    return _IRI_UNSAFE_RE.sub(lambda m: f"%{ord(m.group()):02X}", iri)
21	
22	
23	def make_nq(s_nt: str, p_nt: str, o_nt: str, graph_iri: str) -> NQuad:
24	    """Return one N-Quads line."""
25	    return f"{s_nt} {p_nt} {o_nt} <{graph_iri}> ."
26	
27	
28	def coerce_list(val: object) -> list:
29	    """Normalise None/dict/list to a list."""
30	    if val is None:
31	        return []
32	    if isinstance(val, dict):
33	        return [val]
34	    if isinstance(val, list):
35	        return val
36	    return []
37	
38	
39	def _expand_prefix(curie: str) -> str:
40	    """Expand a CURIE (e.g. 'rdam:P30134') to a full IRI."""
41	    for prefix, base in _PREFIXES.items():
42	        if curie.startswith(prefix + ":"):
43	            return base + curie[len(prefix) + 1:]
44	    return curie
45	
46	
47	def _to_curie(iri: str) -> str:
48	    """Collapse a full IRI to a CURIE using _PREFIXES; return IRI unchanged if no match."""
49	    for prefix, base in _PREFIXES.items():
50	        if iri.startswith(base):
51	            return f"{prefix}:{iri[len(base):]}"
52	    return iri
53	
54	
55	def mint_cho_uri(obj_id: str) -> str:
56	    """Return the minted GeMeA mocho CHO URI for a 32-char DDB object ID (D22)."""
57	    return GEMEA_BASE + obj_id
58	
59	
60	def mint_bare_id(entity_class: str, raw_id: str) -> str:
61	    """Expand a bare 32-char ID to a full URI (D27).
62	
63	    ProvidedCHO bare IDs → DDB item URI.
64	    All other entity types → urn:ddbedm:<ClassName>:<id>.
65	    Full URIs and URNs are returned unchanged.
66	    """
67	    if raw_id.startswith(("http", "urn")):
68	        return raw_id
69	    if entity_class == "ProvidedCHO":
70	        return DDB_ITEM_BASE + raw_id
71	    return f"urn:ddbedm:{entity_class}:{raw_id}"
72	
73	
74	def _escape_literal(s: str) -> str:
75	    """Escape characters illegal in N-Triples/N-Quads literal content."""
76	    return (s.replace("\\", "\\\\")
77	             .replace('"', '\\"')
78	             .replace("\n", "\\n")
79	             .replace("\r", "\\r")
80	             .replace("\t", "\\t"))
81	
82	
83	def value_to_nt_obj(val: object, sani_ctr: Counter | None = None) -> list[str]:
84	    """Convert a JSONL field value to a list of N-Triples object strings.
85	
86	    Handles all value shapes produced by the DDB EDM JSONL:
87	      None / ""                    → []
88	      str (non-empty)              → ['"escaped"']
89	      list                         → recurse and flatten
90	      {"resource": IRI}            → ["<IRI>"] (IRI percent-encoded if unsafe chars present)
91	      {"lang": L, "$": T}          → ['"T"@L']
92	      {"lang": null, "$": T}       → ['"T"']
93	      {"resource": null, "$": ""}  → []
94	
95	    sani_ctr: if provided, incremented once per IRI that required sanitisation.
96	    """
97	    if val is None:
98	        return []
99	    if isinstance(val, str):
100	        return [f'"{_escape_literal(val)}"'] if val else []
101	    if isinstance(val, list):
102	        result = []
103	        for item in val:
104	            result.extend(value_to_nt_obj(item, sani_ctr))
105	        return result
106	    if isinstance(val, dict):
107	        resource = val.get("resource")
108	        if resource:
109	            parts = resource.split()  # split handles multi-URI values (DDB data quality issue)
110	            result = []
111	            for uri in parts:
112	                sanitized = _sanitize_iri(uri)
113	                if sani_ctr is not None and sanitized != uri:
114	                    sani_ctr["uri_sanitized"] += 1
115	                result.append(f"<{sanitized}>")
116	            if sani_ctr is not None and len(parts) > 1:
117	                sani_ctr["uri_split"] += len(parts)
118	            return result
119	        text = val.get("$", "")
120	        if not text:
121	            return []
122	        escaped = _escape_literal(str(text))
123	        lang = val.get("lang")
124	        if lang:
125	            return [f'"{escaped}"@{lang}']
126	        return [f'"{escaped}"']
127	    return []
128	
129	
130	def normalize_date(s: str) -> list[str]:
131	    """Normalise a dc:date string to ISO 8601.
132	
133	    8-digit compact YYYYMMDD → YYYY-MM-DD.
134	    ISO interval begin/end → [begin, end].
135	    All other values returned unchanged.
136	    """
137	    s = s.strip()
138	    if "/" in s:
139	        parts = s.split("/", 1)
140	        return [normalize_date(p)[0] for p in parts]
141	    if len(s) == 8 and s.isdigit():
142	        return [f"{s[:4]}-{s[4:6]}-{s[6:]}"]
143	    return [s]
144	
145	
146	def is_ddb_or_gnd(uri: str) -> bool:
147	    """True if URI is a DDB organization or GND authority URI."""
148	    return uri.startswith((
149	        "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/organization/",
150	        "http://d-nb.info/gnd/",
151	        "https://d-nb.info/gnd/",
152	    ))
153	
154	
155	def resolve_agent(
156	    label: str,
157	    resource: str,
158	    agents_index: dict[str, AgentDict],
159	) -> AgentDict | None:
160	    """Resolve a creator/contributor to an Agent dict.
161	
162	    URI match preferred; label match fallback.
163	    Returns None if no match found.
164	    """
165	    if resource and resource in agents_index:
166	        return agents_index[resource]
167	    if label and label in agents_index:
168	        return agents_index[label]
169	    return None
170	
171	
172	def _extract_mediatype_sector(concepts: object) -> tuple[str, str]:
173	    """Return (mediatype_iri, sector_iri) from the record's Concept list."""
174	    mediatype = "any"
175	    sector    = "any"
176	    for c in coerce_list(concepts):
177	        if not isinstance(c, dict):
178	            continue
179	        about = c.get("about") or ""
180	        if about.startswith(_MEDIATYPE_PREFIX):
181	            mediatype = about
182	        elif about.startswith(_SECTOR_PREFIX):
183	            sector = about
184	    return mediatype, sector
185	
186	
187	def get_object_id(record: dict) -> str | None:
188	    """Extract 32-char object ID from ProvidedCHO.about URI. Returns None on failure."""
189	    try:
190	        about = record["edm"]["RDF"]["ProvidedCHO"]["about"]
191	    except (KeyError, TypeError):
192	        return None
193	    if not about:
194	        return None
195	    # Bare 32-char ID (D27)
196	    if len(about) == 32 and not about.startswith("http"):
197	        return about
198	    obj_id = about.rstrip("/").rsplit("/", 1)[-1]
199	    return obj_id if len(obj_id) == 32 else None
200

1	"""Triple emitters: ddbedm passthrough, mocho alignment, PROV-O, and werk_staging."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from collections import Counter
6	
7	from .constants import (
8	    AgentDict, NQList, PropAlign,
9	    RDF_TYPE, RDFS_LABEL, OWL_SAMEAS,
10	    DCTERMS_SOURCE, DCTERMS_ID, DCTERMS_TYPE, DCTERMS_HAS_VER, DCTERMS_REF,
11	    DCTERMS_RIGHTS, DCTERMS_CREATOR, DCTERMS_SUBJECT,
12	    DC_ID, DC_TITLE, DC_DESCRIPTION, DC_SUBJECT, DC_CONTRIBUTOR,
13	    FOAF_ORG, FOAF_NAME, FOAF_THUMBNAIL,
14	    SCHEMA_URL, MOCHO_ISIL, MOCHO_NS, MOCHO_AGENT, RICO_HAS_RST,

"""Triple emitters: ddbedm passthrough, mocho alignment, PROV-O, and werk_staging."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections import Counter

from .constants import (
    AgentDict, NQList, PropAlign,
    RDF_TYPE, RDFS_LABEL, OWL_SAMEAS,
    DCTERMS_SOURCE, DCTERMS_ID, DCTERMS_TYPE, DCTERMS_HAS_VER, DCTERMS_REF,
    DCTERMS_RIGHTS, DCTERMS_CREATOR, DCTERMS_SUBJECT,
    DC_ID, DC_TITLE, DC_DESCRIPTION, DC_SUBJECT, DC_CONTRIBUTOR,
    FOAF_ORG, FOAF_NAME, FOAF_THUMBNAIL,
    SCHEMA_URL, MOCHO_ISIL, MOCHO_NS, MOCHO_AGENT, RICO_HAS_RST,
    PROV_ENTITY, PROV_AGENT, PROV_SW_AGENT,
    PROV_DERIVED, PROV_ATTRIBUTED, PROV_GEN_TIME, PROV_ON_BEHALF,
    DCAT_DATASET, XSD_DATETIME,
    DDB_BASE, DDB_ITEM_BASE, DDB_API_BASE, EDM_NS,
    _EDM_ENTITY_TYPES, _DDBEDM_PROP, _MOCHO_SKIP, _NEW_NS,
    _CLASS_WEMI, _CONTRIBUTOR_COL, _W_SLOT_CLASSES, SUBJECT_KEYS,
)
from .utils import (
    make_nq, coerce_list, mint_bare_id, _escape_literal, _sanitize_iri,
    value_to_nt_obj, normalize_date, is_ddb_or_gnd, resolve_agent, _to_curie,
)


def emit_ddbedm_triples(rdf: dict, graph_iri: str) -> tuple[NQList, Counter, Counter, Counter]:
    """Emit verbatim EDM passthrough triples for all entity types in rdf (§6.1).

    Subject: first URI in entity['about']; owl:sameAs emitted for any additional URIs.
    Includes mt007 records.
    Returns (lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr, sani_ctr) where sani_ctr tracks
    uri_sanitized, uri_split, and uri_about_split counts.
    """
    lines:     NQList  = []
    class_ctr: Counter = Counter()
    pred_ctr:  Counter = Counter()
    sani_ctr:  Counter = Counter()
    _skip = frozenset({"about"})
    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
        edm_class = _EDM_ENTITY_TYPES.get(entity_type)
        for entity in coerce_list(entities):
            if not isinstance(entity, dict):
                continue
            raw_about = (entity.get("about") or "").strip()
            if not raw_about:
                continue
            about_parts = raw_about.split()
            subj_uri = mint_bare_id(entity_type, _sanitize_iri(about_parts[0]))
            subj_nt  = f"<{subj_uri}>"
            if len(about_parts) > 1:
                sani_ctr["uri_about_split"] += len(about_parts) - 1
                for alt in about_parts[1:]:
                    lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, f"<{OWL_SAMEAS}>",
                                         f"<{_sanitize_iri(alt)}>", graph_iri))
            if edm_class:
                lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{edm_class}>", graph_iri))
                class_ctr[_to_curie(edm_class)] += 1
            for key, val in entity.items():
                if key in _skip:
                    continue
                pred_iri = _DDBEDM_PROP.get(key)
                if not pred_iri:
                    continue
                pred_nt = f"<{pred_iri}>"
                curie   = _to_curie(pred_iri)
                for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(val, sani_ctr):
                    lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, pred_nt, obj_nt, graph_iri))
                    pred_ctr[curie] += 1
    return lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr, sani_ctr


def emit_prov_triples(record: dict, ddb_cho_uri: str, graph_iri: str) -> NQList:
    """Emit PROV-O Layer 1 (Without-Activity) for one record (§6.2, ddbedm-prov-o-plan.md §2)."""
    lines: NQList = []
    props = record.get("properties") or {}
    prov  = record.get("provider-info") or {}

    item_id         = (props.get("item-id")         or "").strip()
    dataset_id      = (props.get("dataset-id")      or "").strip()
    dataset_label   = (props.get("dataset-label")   or "").strip()
    revision        = (props.get("revision-id")     or "").strip()
    ingest_dt       = (props.get("ingest-date")     or "").strip()
    map_ver         = (props.get("mapping-version") or "").strip()
    provider_ddb_id = (prov.get("provider-ddb-id")  or "").strip()
    provider_name   = (prov.get("provider-name")    or "").strip()
    provider_uri    = (prov.get("provider-uri")     or "").strip()
    provider_id     = (prov.get("provider-id")      or "").strip()
    provider_isil   = (prov.get("provider-isil")    or "").strip()

    src_desc  = (record.get("source") or {}).get("description") or {}
    src_ref   = (src_desc.get("record") or {}) if isinstance(src_desc, dict) else {}
    ref_val   = (src_ref.get("ref",  "") or "").strip() if isinstance(src_ref, dict) else ""
    src_href  = (src_ref.get("href", "") or "").strip() if isinstance(src_ref, dict) else ""
    rec_type  = (src_ref.get("type", "") or "").strip() if isinstance(src_ref, dict) else ""

    ds_uri   = f"urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:{dataset_id}"      if dataset_id      else ""
    xslt_uri = f"urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:{map_ver}"    if map_ver         else ""
    prov_uri = f"urn:ddbedm:provider-info:provider-ddb-id:{provider_ddb_id}" \

# goethe-faust — Claude Project Instructions

**Goethe-Faust corpus**: 115,432 DDB records used as the reference implementation
corpus for the mocho ingest pipeline. Scripts and notes here are the primary
development environment for `transform_edm_to_mocho.py` and its dispatch tables.

## Start here

Read `scripts/README.md` for every script: purpose, usage, inputs/outputs,
dependencies. Read `notes/transform-adr.md` for all transform decisions (D1–D12).
Read `notes/transform-script-plan.md` for the full dc:type dispatch architecture.

Key files:
- `scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py` — reference transform (JSONL → N-Triples + JSON-LD)
- `output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` — dc:type × sector → rdf:type dispatch table
- `output/config/image_type2class.json` — mt002 dispatch config
- `notes/transform-adr.md` — authoritative decisions D1–D12
- `notes/image-type-class-mapping.md` — IMAGE dispatch model (D11, D12)
- `data/ddbedm/json_schema_paths.csv` — JSON schema: all leaf paths + observed value types across the corpus

## Conventions

- No hardcoded absolute paths; no hardcoded secrets
- Scripts are stdlib-only unless a dependency is justified in the header
- All data-derived tables have a backing CSV/JSON before being inlined into a note
- Header block on every script: purpose, usage, inputs/outputs, dependencies, assumptions

## DDB Sector (sparte) and Mediatype reference

**Sector (sparte)**

| Code | Sector |
|---|---|
| sparte001 | Archive |
| sparte002 | Library |
| sparte003 | Monument Preservation |
| sparte004 | Research |
| sparte005 | Media Library |
| sparte006 | Museum |
| sparte007 | Others |

**Mediatype (mt)**

| Code | Mediatype |
|---|---|
| mt001 | Audio |
| mt002 | Photo |
| mt003 | Text |
| mt005 | Video |
| mt007 | Not Digitized |

def emit_subject_triples(
    cho_nt: str,
    subject_vals: list,
    concepts_index: dict[str, dict],
    graph_iri: str,
) -> NQList:
    """Emit dcterms:subject (IRI path) or dc:subject (literal path) per value (D1 amended)."""
    lines: NQList = []
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for val in subject_vals:
        if not isinstance(val, dict):
            continue
        resource = (val.get("resource") or "").strip()
        label    = (val.get("$")        or "").strip()
        lang     = (val.get("lang")     or "").strip()
        if resource:
            if resource in seen:
                continue
            seen.add(resource)
            lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_SUBJECT}>", f"<{_sanitize_iri(resource)}>", graph_iri))
            concept = concepts_index.get(resource)
            if concept:
                for pl in coerce_list(concept.get("prefLabel")):
                    for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(pl):
                        lines.append(make_nq(f"<{_sanitize_iri(resource)}>", f"<{RDFS_LABEL}>",
                                             obj_nt, graph_iri))
        elif label:
            key = f"lit:{label}"
            if key in seen:
                continue
            seen.add(key)
            escaped = _escape_literal(label)
            obj_nt  = f'"{escaped}"@{lang}' if lang else f'"{escaped}"'
            lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{DC_SUBJECT}>", obj_nt, graph_iri))
    return lines


def emit_creator_triples(
    cho_nt: str,
    creator_vals: list,
    agents_index: dict[str, AgentDict],
    target_class: str,
    class_prop_align: PropAlign,
    graph_iri: str,
) -> NQList:
    """Emit class-specific creator predicate (Track 1) and dcterms:creator agent stub (Track 2).

    Both tracks run independently for each creator value (D2 / props-mapping §4).
    """
    lines: NQList = []
    dc_creator_iri = "http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/creator"
    track1_prop    = class_prop_align.get((target_class, dc_creator_iri), "")

    for val in coerce_list(creator_vals):
        if not isinstance(val, dict):
            continue
        resource = (val.get("resource") or "").strip()
        label    = (val.get("$")        or "").strip()
        lang     = (val.get("lang")     or "").strip()

        # Track 1: class-specific predicate (always runs when target_prop is known)
        if track1_prop:
            if resource:
                lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{track1_prop}>", f"<{_sanitize_iri(resource)}>", graph_iri))
            elif label:
                escaped = _escape_literal(label)
                obj_nt  = f'"{escaped}"@{lang}' if lang else f'"{escaped}"'
                lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{track1_prop}>", obj_nt, graph_iri))

        # Track 2: generic dcterms:creator + agent stub (D2 — both tracks always run)
        agent = resolve_agent(label, resource, agents_index)
        if agent:
            agent_uri = (agent.get("about") or "").strip()
            if agent_uri and is_ddb_or_gnd(agent_uri):
                lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_CREATOR}>",
                                     f"<{agent_uri}>", graph_iri))
                agent_nt = f"<{agent_uri}>"
                lines.append(make_nq(agent_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{MOCHO_AGENT}>", graph_iri))
                pref = agent.get("prefLabel") or label
                if pref and isinstance(pref, str):
                    lines.append(make_nq(agent_nt, f"<{RDFS_LABEL}>",
                                         f'"{_escape_literal(pref)}"', graph_iri))
    return lines


def emit_contributor_triples(
    cho_nt: str,
    contributor_vals: list,
    event_participant_index: dict[str, str],
    lido_dispatch: dict[str, dict],
    target_class: str,
    wemi: str,
    graph_iri: str,
) -> NQList:
    """Emit contributor triples using LIDO event-type dispatch (D3/D25, props-mapping §5)."""
    lines: NQList = []
    prop_col = _CONTRIBUTOR_COL.get((wemi, target_class), "dc_agent_fallback")

    for val in coerce_list(contributor_vals):
        if not isinstance(val, dict):
            continue
        resource = (val.get("resource") or "").strip()
        label    = (val.get("$")        or "").strip()
        lang     = (val.get("lang")     or "").strip()

        lido_type   = event_participant_index.get(resource) if resource else None
        lido_row    = lido_dispatch.get(lido_type) if lido_type else None
        target_prop = (
            (lido_row.get(prop_col) or lido_row.get("dc_agent_fallback") or DC_CONTRIBUTOR)
            if lido_row else DC_CONTRIBUTOR
        )

        if resource:
            lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{target_prop}>", f"<{_sanitize_iri(resource)}>", graph_iri))
            agent_nt = f"<{_sanitize_iri(resource)}>"
            lines.append(make_nq(agent_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{MOCHO_AGENT}>", graph_iri))
            if label:
                escaped = _escape_literal(label)
                obj_nt  = f'"{escaped}"@{lang}' if lang else f'"{escaped}"'
                lines.append(make_nq(agent_nt, f"<{RDFS_LABEL}>", obj_nt, graph_iri))
        elif label:
            escaped = _escape_literal(label)
            obj_nt  = f'"{escaped}"@{lang}' if lang else f'"{escaped}"'
            lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{DC_CONTRIBUTOR}>", obj_nt, graph_iri))
    return lines

Let me check the constants file and then look at where `hasMet` might be mapped incorrectly to a gemea resource subject.

So there are two problems here—the subject shouldn't be a gemea resource in the first place, and the object isn't being properly resolved to a full URI.

The `edm:hasMet` property is meant to link EDM Events to places or concepts, not CHO resources, so this predicate is being applied to the wrong entity type. I need to check the mocho emit function to understand why it's being generated.

"""Constants: IRIs, prefix tables, dispatch tables, path defaults, and type aliases."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple

# ─── Paths ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SCRIPT_DIR  = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
PROJECT_DIR = SCRIPT_DIR.parents[1]   # scripts/transform/ → scripts/ → project root

DEFAULT_JSONL        = PROJECT_DIR / "data"   / "items-all-goethe-faust.json"
DEFAULT_IDS          = PROJECT_DIR / "data"   / "ids-all-goethe-faust.txt"

DEFAULT_LIDO         = PROJECT_DIR / "output" / "config" / "lido_event_types.csv"

DEFAULT_AUDIO        = PROJECT_DIR / "output" / "config" / "audio_type2class.json"
DEFAULT_OUTPUT_BASE  = PROJECT_DIR / "output" / "transform"

# ─── Type aliases ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NQuad     = str

# ─── Named graphs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

GRAPH_DDBEDM = "https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/ddbedm"

Now I'm planning the actual implementation: I'll add helper functions to build a bare ID to URI mapping and expand object notation, update the skip list in constants, and modify the emitters to use these new utilities.

Before I proceed, I should verify whether the contributor and creator triple emitters also need this expansion—they pull resources from the agents index which should already contain full URIs, so they might not need the same treatment. I see that creators and contributors are handled separately through their own functions rather than the generic loop, so the fix I'm making to `expand_obj_nt` won't affect them—but that's okay since the user hasn't reported issues there. I need to make sure I'm not changing the signatures of `emit_ddbedm_triples` or `emit_subject_triples`, which both look fine as they are.

Now I'm adding `bare_id_to_uri` as an optional keyword argument to pass through the mapping. Since the codebase already imports `from __future__ import annotations`, I can safely use the `dict[str, str] | None` type hint syntax even in Python 3.9.

I'm building the index function to iterate through all entity types in the RDF record and extract bare IDs from the `about` fields, then mapping them to their full URIs. The `expand_obj_nt` function will serve as a simple post-processor to apply these expansions.

Writing the implementation... I see the issue—I need to build the bare ID index before iterating through the RDF data, so I can resolve bare IDs as I emit triples. I'll restructure `emit_ddbedm_triples` to do a pre-pass that builds the index, then use it during the main entity loop.

For `emit_mocho_triples`, I'm applying the same pattern: build the index upfront, then pass it to the object expansion function as I construct the triples.

Now I'm looking at `emit_subject_triples`, which takes the subject values and uses the concepts index along with the bare ID mapping to resolve and emit the appropriate triples.

Continuing with the triple generation...

I'm realizing there's a problem with bare IDs that aren't in the lookup table—they'd still get emitted as invalid URIs. I should add a fallback that mints a proper URI for any bare ID that doesn't have a mapping, using the Concept type as the default since these are subject resources. For object values in the general expansion function, I can't assume the type, so that's trickier, but for subjects specifically I can use this approach.

Looking back at the user's actual error, they're seeing a bare ID that should definitely be in the index if the Concept entity is present in the record, which it almost certainly is. So the real issue might be elsewhere—maybe the index isn't being built correctly or the bare ID format is different than expected. I should verify the index is capturing these IDs properly before adding fallbacks.

Now I'm ready to implement the fix. I'll add the bare ID indexing and expansion logic to utils, update the skip list in constants to include "hasMet", and wire it all together in the emitter with minimal changes.

I have the full picture now. Both bugs:

1. **Bare ID not expanded**: `value_to_nt_obj` wraps resource values in `<...>` without calling `mint_bare_id`. The `isPartOf` fix (lines 627–640) is the pattern — it wasn't generalized. Affects both graphs, and `emit_subject_triples` too.

2. **`edm:hasMet` on gemea CHO**: `hasMet` is in `_DDBEDM_PROP` but not in `_MOCHO_SKIP`, so the generic property loop in `emit_mocho_triples` emits it on the minted gemea URI. It's an EDM Event property with no mocho alignment.

Three-part fix: add `build_bare_id_index`/`expand_obj_nt` to `utils.py`, wire them into `emit_ddbedm_triples` and the generic loop + subject emitter in `emit_mocho_triples`, and add `hasMet` to `_MOCHO_SKIP`.

"""
Purpose:    Unit tests for the transform package.
Usage:      pytest scripts/transform/tests/ -q
Deps:       pytest
Assumes:    Run from project root (goethe-faust/).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import sys
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

# ── Package import ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]))  # adds scripts/ to sys.path

from transform.constants import GRAPH_MOCHO, MOCHO_NS, PROJECT_DIR
from transform.utils import (
    coerce_list,
    make_nq,
    mint_bare_id,
    mint_cho_uri,
    normalize_date,
    value_to_nt_obj,
    _escape_literal,
    get_object_id,
)
from transform.emitters import (
    retype_entities,
    emit_creator_triples,
    emit_contributor_triples,
    emit_subject_triples,
    emit_aggregation_triples,
    emit_place_stubs,
    werk_staging_row,
)
from transform.loaders import load_mediatype_class, load_htype_map

# Config table paths
_CONFIG = PROJECT_DIR / "output" / "config"


# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _rdf_types(lines: list[str]) -> set[str]:
    """Extract the object IRIs from all rdf:type triples in a list of N-Quads lines."""
    rdf_type = "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type"
    result: set[str] = set()
    for line in lines:
        parts = line.split()
        if len(parts) >= 4 and f"<{rdf_type}>" in parts[1]:
            obj = parts[2].strip("<>")
            result.add(obj)
    return result


def _predicates(lines: list[str]) -> set[str]:
    result: set[str] = set()
    for line in lines:
        parts = line.split()
        if len(parts) >= 4:
            result.add(parts[1].strip("<>"))
    return result


def _load_configs():
    mc_map = load_mediatype_class(_CONFIG / "lookup_mediatype_class.csv")
    ht_map = load_htype_map(_CONFIG / "lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv")
    return mc_map, ht_map


# ── normalize_date ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestNormalizeDate:
    def test_compact_yyyymmdd(self):
        assert normalize_date("19870315") == ["1987-03-15"]

    def test_iso_interval(self):
        result = normalize_date("1900/1950")

# ── emit_subject_triples ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestEmitSubjectTriples:
    _cho_nt = "<https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/mocho/" + "F" * 32 + ">"

    def test_iri_subject(self):
        vals = [{"resource": "http://d-nb.info/gnd/4018197-4", "$": "Faust", "lang": "de"}]
        lines = emit_subject_triples(self._cho_nt, vals, {}, GRAPH_MOCHO)
        preds = _predicates(lines)
        assert "http://purl.org/dc/terms/subject" in preds

    def test_literal_subject(self):
        vals = [{"resource": "", "$": "Goethe", "lang": "de"}]
        lines = emit_subject_triples(self._cho_nt, vals, {}, GRAPH_MOCHO)
        preds = _predicates(lines)
        assert "http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/subject" in preds

    def test_dedup(self):
        uri = "http://d-nb.info/gnd/4018197-4"
        vals = [
            {"resource": uri, "$": "Faust", "lang": "de"},
            {"resource": uri, "$": "Faust", "lang": "de"},
        ]
        lines = emit_subject_triples(self._cho_nt, vals, {}, GRAPH_MOCHO)
        dcterms_sub_lines = [l for l in lines if "terms/subject" in l]
        assert len(dcterms_sub_lines) == 1


# ── emit_place_stubs ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestEmitPlaceStubs:
    def test_emits_label(self):
        places = [{"about": "http://example.org/place/1",
                   "prefLabel": [{"$": "Weimar", "lang": "de"}]}]
        lines = emit_place_stubs(places, GRAPH_MOCHO)
        assert any('"Weimar"@de' in nq for nq in lines)

    def test_no_about_skipped(self):
        places = [{"prefLabel": [{"$": "Weimar", "lang": "de"}]}]
        lines = emit_place_stubs(places, GRAPH_MOCHO)
        assert lines == []

    def test_none_input(self):
        assert emit_place_stubs(None, GRAPH_MOCHO) == []


# ── werk_staging_row ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestWerkStagingRow:
    _cho_uri = "https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/mocho/" + "G" * 32
    _rdac_c10001 = "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/C10001"
    _rdac_c10007 = "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/C10007"

    def test_w_slot_returns_row(self):
        cho = {"title": {"$": "Faust", "lang": "de"},
               "creator": [{"resource": "http://d-nb.info/gnd/118540238", "$": "Goethe"}]}
        row = werk_staging_row(self._cho_uri, cho, self._rdac_c10001)
        assert row is not None
        assert row["dc_title"] == "Faust"
        assert "http://d-nb.info/gnd/118540238" in row["creator_uris"]

    def test_m_slot_returns_none(self):
        cho = {"title": {"$": "Faust"}}
        row = werk_staging_row(self._cho_uri, cho, self._rdac_c10007)
        assert row is None

    def test_obj_id_extracted(self):
        cho = {}
        row = werk_staging_row(self._cho_uri, cho, self._rdac_c10001)
        assert row["ddb_obj_id"] == "G" * 32


# ── make_nq ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_make_nq_format():
    line = make_nq("<http://s>", "<http://p>", '"o"', "https://graph/g")
    assert line == '<http://s> <http://p> "o" <https://graph/g> .'

content.text:
All 60 tests pass (including 16 new ones). Now addressing your question about where `_MOCHO_SKIP` values came from.

---

**Re: where did `_MOCHO_SKIP` values come from?**

Looking at [constants.py:99–106](goethe-faust/scripts/transform/constants.py):

```python
_MOCHO_SKIP = frozenset({
    "about", "hierarchyType",
    "creator", "contributor",
    "dcSubject", "dcTermsSubject", "dcTermSubject",
    "dcType",
    "aggregationEntity", "hierarchyPosition",
})
```

Each entry was added for a distinct reason:

| Key | Why skipped in mocho graph |
|---|---|
| `about` | It's the entity subject, not a property |
| `hierarchyType` | DDB-internal structural field (D27); handled separately by `retype_entities` |
| `creator`, `contributor` | Handled by dedicated emitters (`emit_creator_triples`, `emit_contributor_triples`) with LIDO dispatch |
| `dcSubject`, `dcTermsSubject`, `dcTermSubject` | Handled by `emit_subject_triples` with Concept lookup |
| `dcType` | Consumed by `retype_entities` for class dispatch; not re-emitted |
| `aggregationEntity`, `hierarchyPosition` | DDB-internal structural fields with no mocho mapping |

`hasMet` I just added: it's in `_DDBEDM_PROP` (so it passes through to the ddbedm graph correctly), but it has no mocho alignment — so without the skip, the generic loop emitted it raw as `edm:hasMet` on the gemea CHO URI.

def retype_entities(
    sector: str,
    mediatype: str,
    htype_code: str | None,
    dctype_vals: list,
    cho_nt: str,
    mediatype_class_map: dict,
    htype_map: dict,
    audio_type2class: dict,
    graph_iri: str,
) -> tuple[NQList, str, str, dict]:
    """Dispatch rdf:type triples for a ProvidedCHO (§6.4, transform-revised-plan.md §1.1).

    Returns (lines, target_class_full_iri, wemi, dispatch_flags).
    target_class is used for property alignment lookup; wemi for contributor column selection.
    dispatch_flags: {"htype_used": bool, "fallback": bool}
    """
    lines: NQList = []
    row = (
        mediatype_class_map.get((sector, mediatype))
        or mediatype_class_map.get(("any", "any"))
        or {}
    )
    use_htype  = row.get("use_htype", False)
    rdf_type_w = row.get("rdf_type_w", "")
    rdf_type_m = row.get("rdf_type_m", "")

    primary_class = ""
    htype_used    = False

    # Layer 1: htype-derived class (for htype-first strata)
    if use_htype and htype_code:
        entry = htype_map.get(htype_code)
        if entry:
            type_iris, rst_iris = entry
            for t in type_iris:
                lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{t}>", graph_iri))
            for r in rst_iris:
                lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{RICO_HAS_RST}>", f"<{r}>", graph_iri))
            primary_class = type_iris[0]  # first = most specific
            htype_used    = True

    # Layer 2: fixed mediatype class (always emit if set; added on top of layer 1)
    if rdf_type_w:
        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{rdf_type_w}>", graph_iri))
        if not primary_class:
            primary_class = rdf_type_w
    if rdf_type_m:
        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{rdf_type_m}>", graph_iri))
        if not primary_class:
            primary_class = rdf_type_m

    # Audio group dispatch: dc:type → mo:MusicalManifestation (Group A) (§2.3)
    _aco_audio = "https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioManifestation"
    _mo_mani   = "http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalManifestation"
    if rdf_type_m == _aco_audio and dctype_vals and not use_htype:
        for dct in dctype_vals:
            dc_text = (dct.get("$") or "").strip() if isinstance(dct, dict) else ""
            if audio_type2class.get((sector, dc_text)) == "A":
                lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{_mo_mani}>", graph_iri))
                primary_class = _mo_mani
                break

    # D9 fallback — no class resolved
    is_fallback = not primary_class
    if is_fallback:
        fallback = MOCHO_NS + "Manifestation"
        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{fallback}>", graph_iri))
        primary_class = fallback

    wemi = _CLASS_WEMI.get(primary_class, "M")
    return lines, primary_class, wemi, {"htype_used": htype_used, "fallback": is_fallback}

10:Both sector and mediatype are resolved by the transform (Mode B — see ADR §7.2). The transform reads `edm.RDF.Concept[].about`, identifies the vocnet IRI, emits two bridge triples on the CHO, and directly asserts the domain class `rdf:type` (D9, per R1). Pattern 1 `SubClassOf` axioms route to the mocho WEMI pivot via pre-materialisation.
15:  → assert <cho> mocho:sector    vocnet-sparte:sparte002
16:  → assert <cho> mocho:mediaType vocnet-mtype:mt003
23:  → assert <cho> mocho:sector    vocnet-sparte:sparte006
24:  → assert <cho> mocho:mediaType vocnet-mtype:mt002
39:Prefix(vocnet-sparte:=<http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/>)
40:Prefix(vocnet-mtype:=<http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/>)
50:Declaration(NamedIndividual(vocnet-sparte:sparte001))

52:Declaration(NamedIndividual(vocnet-sparte:sparte005))
53:Declaration(NamedIndividual(vocnet-sparte:sparte006))
54:Declaration(NamedIndividual(vocnet-mtype:mt001))
55:Declaration(NamedIndividual(vocnet-mtype:mt002))
56:Declaration(NamedIndividual(vocnet-mtype:mt005))
76:AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:comment <https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#mediatype> "Links a DDB ProvidedCHO to its mediatype (medientyp) skos:Concept. ProvidedCHO.edmType (edm:type) carries one of five uppercase literals (IMAGE, SOUND, TEXT, VIDEO, 3D) — a simple hardcoded lookup resolves these to the vocnet IRI. The transform currently reads from edm.RDF.Concept[].about instead. The mocho:mediaType bridge triple makes the IRI directly queryable on the CHO."@en)

The transform reads `edm.RDF.Concept[].about`, identifies the vocnet IRI, emits two bridge triples on the CHO, and directly asserts the domain class `rdf:type` (D9, per R1). Pattern 1 `SubClassOf` axioms route to the mocho WEMI pivot via pre-materialisation.
15:  → assert <cho> mocho:sector    vocnet-sparte:sparte002
16:  → assert <cho> mocho:mediaType vocnet-mtype:mt003
23:  → assert <cho> mocho:sector    vocnet-sparte:sparte006
24:  → assert <cho> mocho:mediaType vocnet-mtype:mt002

52:Declaration(NamedIndividual(vocnet-sparte:sparte005))
53:Declaration(NamedIndividual(vocnet-sparte:sparte006))

55:Declaration(NamedIndividual(vocnet-mtype:mt002))

76:AnnotationAssertion(rdfs:comment <https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#mediatype> "Links a DDB ProvidedCHO to its mediatype (medientyp) skos:Concept. ProvidedCHO.edmType (edm:type) carries one of five uppercase literals (IMAGE, SOUND, TEXT, VIDEO, 3D) — a simple hardcoded lookup resolves these to the vocnet IRI. The transform currently reads from edm.RDF.Concept[].about instead. The mocho:mediaType bridge triple makes the IRI directly queryable on the CHO."@en)

111:| ClassAssertion + bilingual labels for 7 vocnet-htype individuals | ADD | 21 axioms |

/Users/mta/Documents/claude/transcripts/claude-claude-was-forced-quit-how-to-recover-pr-20260420-115640.md:8790:  "content": "# Plan: Write goethe-faust/notes/transform-revised-plan.md

## Context

The transform work has accumulated across five overlapping notes (`transform-plan.md`, `transform-adr.md`, `transform-script-plan.md`, `transform-edm2mocho-plan.md`, `entity-property-mapping-plan.md`). The goal is one clean, current document — `transform-revised-plan.md` — that captures what is done, what remains, and what each entity type maps to. The old notes stay as history; the revised plan is the live reference.

---

## What already exists (do not re-derive)

| Artifact | Path | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Transform script | `scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py` | Partially implemented |
| Property alignment CSV | `output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` | Complete (may have wrong WEMI rows) |
| htype → RiC-O lookup | `output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | Complete |
| dc:type → class lookup | `output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` | Complete (1,647 rows) |
| Per-entity mapping decisions | `notes/entity-property-mapping.md` | Approved |
| Archival object model | `mocho/notes/archival-objects.md` | Complete |
| ADR D1–D12 | `notes/transform-adr.md` | Accepted |

**Script state**: `_extract_mediatype_sector()`, `_dctype_lookup()`, `load_dctype_map()` exist in the script but `retype_entities()` does not yet call them. No entity-type whitelists beyond ProvidedCHO.creator and ProvidedCHO.contributor.

---

## Content of transform-revised-plan.md

### §1 — Pipeline overview

Brief description: JSONL → transform → N-Triples. Input: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` (115,432 records). Output: `goethe-faust-mocho.nt`. Three mapping layers (property alignment, htype dispatch, dc:type dispatch). Reference `transform-writeup.md` for the Mermaid flowchart.

### §2 — Completed work

- ADR D1–D12 accepted; lookup tables generated; ProvidedCHO fully mapped
- Script infrastructure in place (dispatch functions written, alignment CSV loaded)
- Run result: 44.4M triples, 115,432 records (2026-04-20 baseline)

### §3 — Entity type status table

| Entity type | Instances | Property mapping | rdf:type | Impl status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ProvidedCHO | 115,432 | alignment CSV + creator/contributor whitelists | mocho:Manifestation + dc:type dispatch + htype | ✅ done |
| Aggregation | 115,432 | alignment CSV (EDM-structural stays in_mocho=False) | — | ✅ done |
| WebResource | 312,538 | creator→rdam:P30263; type→rdam:P30002 (whitelists); dcTermsRights/edmRights from CSV | mt002: mocho:ImageObject + rdac:C10007 | ☐ pending |
| Agent | 422,026 | rdaa: whitelists + skos:prefLabel/altLabel/note + owl:sameAs; date corrections | — (defer) | ☐ pending |
| Place | 118,088 | geo:lat/long/alt + skos:prefLabel/altLabel/note + owl:sameAs | — (defer) | ☐ pending |
| PhysicalThing | 55,771 | title→rdaw:P10088; isPartOf→rico:includesOrIncluded | htype dispatch only (no mocho:Manifestation) | ☐ pending |
| TimeSpan | 99,930 | begin→schema:startDate; end→schema:endDate | — (defer) | ☐ pending |
| Concept | 717,638 | prefLabel→skos:prefLabel; notation→skos:notation | skos:Concept + type-dispatch | ☐ needs own section |
| Event | 158,407 | all deferred (CRM not in mocho) | — | ❌ deferred |

### §4 — Concept entities (own section)

Concept nodes in `edm.RDF.Concept[]` serve multiple roles — the `about` IRI reveals which:

| IRI prefix | Role | Emit as |
|---|---|---|
| `ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/` | Mediatype vocab term | `skos:Concept` + `skos:prefLabel` + `skos:notation` |
| `ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/` | Sector vocab term | `skos:Concept` + `skos:prefLabel` + `skos:notation` |
| `ddb.vocnet.org/hierarchietyp/` | htype vocab term | already declared in mocho as named individuals — skip or emit labels only |
| GND/VIAF/etc. IRI | Subject/agent/place authority | `skos:Concept` + `skos:prefLabel` (if present) |
| Other / unknown | Generic controlled term | `skos:Concept` + available properties |

Dispatch: inspect `about` IRI prefix at emit time; emit `rdf:type skos:Concept` for all. The `prefLabel` and `notation` keys map to `skos:prefLabel` and `skos:notation`. No additional whitelist keys needed — both are already plain literal values.

This resolves the conflict in earlier notes: Concepts ARE emitted; the current transform-writeup §3 is stale and will be updated.

### §5 — Implementation steps (ordered)

1. **patch_alignment_inmocho.py** — set `in_mocho=False` for wrong-WEMI-level rows in `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` for Agent (date, hasPart, isPartOf, type), Place (hasPart, isPartOf, type), PhysicalThing (title candidates except rdaw:P10088). Write script, run against CSV, verify row counts.

2. **transform_edm_to_mocho.py — wire dc:type dispatch** — call `_dctype_lookup()` inside `retype_entities()` for ProvidedCHO; emit W-slot or M-slot domain class alongside `mocho:Manifestation`.

3. **transform_edm_to_mocho.py — mt002 WebResource typing** — inside `retype_entities()`, when mediatype==mt002, emit `mocho:ImageObject`, `rdac:C10007`, `rdam:P30001 rdact:1018`, `vra:imageOf <cho-uri>` for each WebResource.

4. **transform_edm_to_mocho.py — entity-type whitelists** — add per-type whitelist dicts (loaded from inline constants or small JSON configs):
   - WebResource: `creator`→`rdam:P30263`, `type`→`rdam:P30002`
   - Agent: `name`→`rdaa:P50102`, `begin`→`rdaa:P50067`, `end`→`rdaa:P50068`, `dateOfBirth`→`rdaa:P50067`, `dateOfDeath`→`rdaa:P50068`, `dateOfEstablishment`→`rdaa:P50067`, `dateOfTermination`→`rdaa:P50068`, `gender`→`rdaa:P50116`, `biographicalInformation`→`rdaa:P50113`, `professionOrOccupation`→`rdaa:P50100`, `placeOfBirth`→`rdaa:P50069`, `placeOfDeath`→`rdaa:P50070`, `note`→`skos:note`, `sameAs`→`owl:sameAs`, `prefLabel`→`skos:prefLabel`, `altLabel`→`skos:altLabel`
   - Place: `lat`→`geo:lat`, `long`→`geo:long`, `alt`→`geo:alt`, `prefLabel`→`skos:prefLabel`, `altLabel`→`skos:altLabel`, `note`→`skos:note`, `sameAs`→`owl:sameAs`
   - PhysicalThing: `title`→`rdaw:P10088`, `isPartOf`→`rico:includesOrIncluded`
   - TimeSpan: `begin`→`schema:startDate`, `end`→`schema:endDate`

5. **transform_edm_to_mocho.py — Concept emission** — add Concept to the entity emit loop: for each Concept node emit `rdf:type skos:Concept`, `skos:prefLabel` (from `prefLabel`), `skos:notation` (from `notation`). IRI prefix dispatch for type-specific handling (skip htype individuals already declared in mocho).

6. **transform-adr.md** — add D13–D1N decisions for each new entity type (Agent rdaa:, Place geo:, WebResource type whitelist, PhysicalThing rico:includesOrIncluded correction, TimeSpan schema:, Concept emit).

7. **scripts/README.md** — add entries for `patch_alignment_inmocho.py` and any new scripts.

8. **transform-writeup.md §3** — update stale Concept section to reflect emission.

### §6 — Files to create/modify

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `goethe-faust/notes/transform-revised-plan.md` | **Create** — this document |
| `goethe-faust/scripts/patch_alignment_inmocho.py` | **Create** |
| `goethe-faust/scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py` | **Modify** — steps 2–5 |
| `goethe-faust/notes/transform-adr.md` | **Modify** — add D13+ |
| `goethe-faust/scripts/README.md` | **Modify** |
| `goethe-faust/notes/transform-writeup.md` | **Modify** — §3 Concept section |

### §7 — Verification

After steps 1–5:
- Run transform on full corpus; compare triple count to 44.4M baseline (expect significant increase from Concept + Agent + Place emission)
- Spot-check: one Agent entity — verify `rdaa:P50067` (birth date) present
- Spot-check: one Place entity — verify `geo:lat` present
- Spot-check: one mt002 WebResource — verify `mocho:ImageObject` + `vra:imageOf` present
- Spot-check: one Concept with medientyp IRI — verify `skos:Concept` + `skos:prefLabel` present
- Spot-check: one PhysicalThing with htype_030 — verify `rico:RecordSet` + `rico:includesOrIncluded` used (not `rico:isOrWasPartOf`)
- Check `transform_stats.json` output for per-entity-type triple counts

---

## Note on old plan files

`transform-plan.md`, `transform-script-plan.md`, `transform-edm2mocho-plan.md`, `entity-property-mapping-plan.md` remain as history. `transform-revised-plan.md` supersedes them as the live reference. Cross-references in CLAUDE.md should point to the revised plan.

/Users/mta/Documents/claude/transcripts/claude-claude-was-forced-quit-how-to-recover-pr-20260420-115640.md:8860:  "content": "# Transform revised plan: EDM → mocho N-Triples

**Date**: 2026-04-29  
**Status**: In progress  
**Supersedes**: `transform-plan.md`, `transform-script-plan.md`, `transform-edm2mocho-plan.md`, `entity-property-mapping-plan.md`  
**Related**: `transform-adr.md` (D1–D12), `entity-property-mapping.md`, `transform-writeup.md`

---

## 1. Pipeline overview

Input: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` (115,432 records, JSONL).  
Output: `goethe-faust-mocho.nt` (N-Triples).  
Script: `scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py`.

Three mapping layers — see `transform-writeup.md` for the Mermaid flowchart:

1. **Property alignment** — `output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` keyed by `(entity_type, json_key)` → predicate IRI. Only `in_mocho=True` rows emitted. Per-type whitelists bypass the table for fan-out or wrong-level properties.
2. **htype dispatch** — `output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` → RiC-O class + dual `rico:hasRecordSetType` for archival hierarchy nodes.
3. **dc:type dispatch** — `output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` (1,647 rows) keyed by `(mediatype, sector, dc_type_de)` with three-level fallback → W-slot or M-slot domain class.

---

## 2. Completed work

- ADR D1–D12 accepted (`transform-adr.md`)
- All lookup tables generated and verified:
  - `output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` — complete
  - `output/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` — complete
  - `output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` — complete, 1,647 rows, 0 TBDs
- Script infrastructure in place: `_extract_mediatype_sector()`, `_dctype_lookup()`, `load_dctype_map()`, `load_htype_map()`, `load_alignment()` — all written
- ProvidedCHO and Aggregation fully mapped
- Baseline run (2026-04-20): 44.4M triples, 115,432 records

---

## 3. Entity type status

| Entity type | Instances | Property mapping | rdf:type | Impl status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ProvidedCHO | 115,432 | alignment CSV + creator/contributor whitelists | `mocho:Manifestation` + dc:type dispatch + htype | ✅ done |
| Aggregation | 115,432 | alignment CSV (EDM-structural `in_mocho=False` per D4) | — | ✅ done |
| WebResource | 312,538 | creator→`rdam:P30263`; type→`rdam:P30002` (whitelists); dcTermsRights/edmRights from CSV | mt002: `mocho:ImageObject` + `rdac:C10007` | ☐ pending |
| Agent | 422,026 | rdaa: whitelists + skos: labels + `owl:sameAs`; wrong-WEMI rows corrected | — (defer) | ☐ pending |
| Place | 118,088 | `geo:lat/long/alt` + skos: labels + `owl:sameAs` | — (defer) | ☐ pending |
| PhysicalThing | 55,771 | `title`→`rdaw:P10088`; `isPartOf`→`rico:includesOrIncluded` | htype dispatch only (no `mocho:Manifestation`) | ☐ pending |
| TimeSpan | 99,930 | `begin`→`schema:startDate`; `end`→`schema:endDate` | — (defer) | ☐ pending |
| Concept | 717,638 | `prefLabel`→`skos:prefLabel`; `notation`→`skos:notation` | `skos:Concept` + IRI-prefix dispatch | ☐ pending |
| Event | 158,407 | all deferred (CRM not in mocho) | — | ❌ deferred |

---

## 4. Concept entities

Concept nodes in `edm.RDF.Concept[]` serve multiple roles. The `about` IRI prefix determines the role:

| IRI prefix | Role | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/` | Mediatype vocab term | emit `skos:Concept` + `skos:prefLabel` + `skos:notation` |
| `ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/` | Sector vocab term | emit `skos:Concept` + `skos:prefLabel` + `skos:notation` |
| `ddb.vocnet.org/hierarchietyp/` | htype vocab term | skip (already declared as named individuals in mocho) |
| GND / VIAF / other authority | Subject / agent / place ref | emit `skos:Concept` + `skos:prefLabel` if present |
| Unknown / other | Generic controlled term | emit `skos:Concept` + available properties |

All Concept nodes: emit `rdf:type skos:Concept`. Properties `prefLabel` → `skos:prefLabel` (lang-tagged), `notation` → `skos:notation`.

---

## 5. Implementation steps

### 5.1 Patch alignment CSV

**Script**: `scripts/patch_alignment_inmocho.py` (new)  
Set `in_mocho=False` for wrong-WEMI-level rows:
- `Agent`: `date` (keep only `rdaa:P50039`), `hasPart`, `isPartOf`, `type` — all WEMI candidates
- `Place`: `hasPart`, `isPartOf`, `type` — all WEMI candidates
- `PhysicalThing`: `title` candidates except `rdaw:P10088`

Run against `output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv`; verify row counts before/after.

### 5.2 Wire dc:type dispatch in retype_entities()

Call `_dctype_lookup(mediatype, sector, dc_type_de)` inside `retype_entities()` for ProvidedCHO. Emit W-slot class instead of `mocho:Manifestation` (or M-slot class alongside it). Functions already exist — just needs to be called.

### 5.3 mt002 WebResource typing

Inside `retype_entities()`, when `mediatype == mt002`, for each WebResource URI emit:
```
<wr-uri>  rdf:type  mocho:ImageObject
<wr-uri>  rdf:type  rdac:C10007          # RDA Manifestation
<wr-uri>  rdam:P30001  rdact:1018        # has carrier type
<wr-uri>  vra:imageOf  <cho-uri>
```

### 5.5 Concept emission

Add Concept to the entity emit loop in `transform_record()`. For each Concept node:
1. Emit `rdf:type skos:Concept`
2. Emit `skos:prefLabel` from `prefLabel` key (lang-tagged `@de` if present)
3. Emit `skos:notation` from `notation` key
4. IRI-prefix check: skip htype individuals (`ddb.vocnet.org/hierarchietyp/`)

### 5.6 ADR update

Add D13–D1N to `notes/transform-adr.md` covering:
